title: DOJ Withdrew From Biden-Era Suit Demanding Race-Based Election Maps
description: DOJ withdrew from a Biden-era lawsuit that aimed to require Alabama to use race-based election maps. Confirming that DOJ's position is correct, the Supreme Court ruled in Louisiana v. Callais that drawing election maps based on race is unconstitutional under the Fourteenth Amendment, which guarantees equal protection of the law to all Americans.

title: DOJ Filed a Statement Demanding Judges Not Force States to Draw Race-Based Voting Maps
description: DOJ filed a statement of interest in multiple lawsuits that seek to force the state of Alabama to use race-based maps for its congressional elections. Those lawsuits claim that a federal judge must "pre-clear" election maps under the Voting Rights Act, and DOJ argued that this is not legally necessary or permitted.

title: DOJ Inspected Federal Election Records to Ensure Only Eligible Americans Vote With Alabama's Support
description: Under the Civil Rights Act of 1960, DOJ is responsible to protect against state malfeasance with respect to all records requisite to voting and may inspect state-held federal election records to ensure their integrity. DOJ requested Alabama's records, and the state cooperated and provided them.

title: DOJ Ensures Primary Election Military Ballots Are Sent Early to Comply With Federal Law
description: DOJ works for months to ensure all military voters' ballots are counted. DOJ meets with election officials in Alabama months before election day to ensure the State guarantees all military voters are sent ballots not later than 45 days before the election.

title: DOJ Warns State Officials of Their Criminal Liability Regarding Illegal Voting
description: DOJ notified state officials of their duty to prevent illegal voting. DOJ reminded them that it is a crime to intentionally allow ineligible persons to vote or conspire to interfere with another person's constitutional rights.

title: DOJ Sent Arizona a Letter Demanding the State Require Voters Provide ID Information When They Register
description: Section 303 of the Help America Vote Act requires voter registration applicants provide personal identifying information for voter verification. DOJ sent Arizona a letter demanding change to its voter registration application to reflect this requirement.

title: DOJ Will Send Election Monitors to Maricopa County to Ensure Federal Law Compliance
description: DOJ will send monitors to Maricopa County to monitor operations. description: California has more active military servicemembers than any other state. DOJ meets with election officials in California months before election day to ensure the State guarantees all military voters are sent ballots not later than 45 days before the election.

title: DOJ Criminally Prosecutes California Woman for Paying People to Register to Vote
description: 52 U.S.C. 10307(c) makes it a felony to pay someone to register to vote, but California woman Brenda Lee Brown Armstrong did it anyway. Armstrong paid homeless people living on Los Angeles' Skid Row to register so she could collect more paid signatures for petition campaigns. DOJ brought charges against her, and she pled guilty.

title: President Trump Restores "Justice for All" to the Justice Department
description: Shortly after President Trump took office, President Trump and DOJ leadership made it clear that DOJ's mission is to do justice to all citizens, not to dispense privileges to favored groups. "The scales of justice will be rebalanced. The vicious, violent, and unfair weaponization of the Justice Department and our government will end." January 2025 Inaugural Address.

title: DOJ Withdrew From Biden-Era Suit Demanding Race-Based Election Maps
description: DOJ withdrew from a Biden-era lawsuit that aimed to require Texas to use race-based election maps. Confirming that DOJ's position is correct, the Supreme Court ruled in Louisiana v. Callais that drawing election maps based on race is unconstitutional under the Fourteenth Amendment, which guarantees equal protection of the law to all Americans.

title: DOJ Warns State Officials of Their Criminal Liability Regarding Illegal Voting
description: DOJ notified state officials of their duty to prevent illegal voting. DOJ reminded them that it is a crime to intentionally allow ineligible persons to vote or conspire to interfere with another person's constitutional rights.

title: DOJ Sued Utah for Failing to Provide Federal Election Records
description: Under the Civil Rights Act of 1960, DOJ is responsible to protect against malfeasance with respect to all records requisite to voting and may inspect state-held federal election records to ensure their integrity. DOJ requested Utah's records, but the state refused to provide them. DOJ then sued to obtain the records.

title: DOJ Will Send Election Monitors to Fairfax County to Ensure Federal Law Compliance
description: DOJ will send election monitors to Fairfax County to monitor operations. This is part of DOJ's efforts to make sure state officials maintain records, prevent illegal voting, and keep elections free and fair—required by the Civil Rights Act of 1960, the Voting Rights Act of 1965, the National Voter Registration Act of 1993, and the Help America Vote Act of 2002.

title: DOJ Filed a Statement Demanding Federal Courts Not Block Wyoming's Voter ID Law
description: DOJ filed a statement of interest in Equality State Policy Center v. Gray. The statement explains that it is constitutional for Wyoming to require ID from voters when they register.

title: DOJ Sent Wyoming a Letter Demanding the State Add All Required Fields to Its Voter Registration
description: DOJ sent a letter notifying Wyoming that its statewide voter registration list must contain all fields.
